title: Retrieving projects | SonarQube Cloud Documentation
content:
# Retrieving projects

## [Retrieving any public or private project](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/managing-your-projects/retrieving-projects/#any-project "Retrieving any public or private project")

1. In the top navigation bar, select the magnifier icon. The **Search for projects...** box is displayed along with a list of pre-selected projects.

2. You can enter a part of the project name in the box to filter the search.

3. Select the project in the list.

![](https://docs.sonarsource.com/Base64-Image-Removed)

![](https://assets-eu-01.kc-usercontent.com:443/b1eeb429-d9e0-0100-be87-468f6802040a/f1f6ae1a-fe10-4e45-90fb-1f3123310cdc/sonarqube-cloud-search-for-projects_Cs0033.png?w=815&h=404&auto=format&fit=crop)

## [Retrieving the projects of your organization](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/managing-your-projects/retrieving-projects/#organization-projects "Retrieving the projects of your organization")

1\. Retrieve your organization:

- Select your account menu in the top right corner of the SonarQube Cloud interface.
- In the menu, under **My Organizations**, select your organization. The organization's **Projects** page opens.

![](https://docs.sonarsource.com/Base64-Image-Removed)

![](https://assets-eu-01.kc-usercontent.com:443/b1eeb429-d9e0-0100-be87-468f6802040a/92a9daf4-fed7-44af-a3a7-97b27208f70f/sonarqube-cloud-organization-page_Cs0003.png?w=1047&h=364&auto=format&fit=crop)

2\. In the left sidebar, you can define filter conditions.

3\. Above the list, you can use the search box to search by project name.

4\. Click the project name hyperlink to open the project.

## [Retrieving your favorite projects](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/managing-your-projects/retrieving-projects/#your-projects "Retrieving your favorite projects")

To mark a project as favorite, see [Managing your project as a developer](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/managing-your-projects/managing-your-project-as-developer/ "Managing your project as a developer").

To retrieve your favorite projects:

1. In the top navigation bar, select **My Projects**. Your favorite projects are listed on the page.

2. In the left sidebar, you can define filter conditions.

3. Above the list, you can use the search box to search by project name.

4. Click the project name hyperlink to open the project.

## [Exploring open-source projects](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/managing-your-projects/retrieving-projects/#any-public-project "Exploring open-source projects")

To explore open-source projects:

- Select **Explore** in the top navigation bar of the SonarQube Cloud UI.
- Or go to `sonarcloud.io/explore/projects`.

## [Viewing and copying project information](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/managing-your-projects/retrieving-projects/#viewing-project-information "Viewing and copying project information")

1. Retrieve the project as explained above.

2. In the left navigation bar, select **Information**. The **Information** page opens.

The page displays:

- The quality gate currently being used for the project.
- The quality profiles currently being used for the project. If the project contains multiple languages, the profile for each language is shown.
- The project and organization keys.
  You can copy them for pasting.
- The last analysis method: the method used for this project's most recent analysis. If the last analysis was done by automatic analysis, this section will display Analyzed by SonarQube Cloud. If the last analysis was done by CI-based analysis, the CI system used will be indicated.

The page allows you to:

- [Subscribe to notifications](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/managing-your-projects/managing-your-project-as-developer/ "Subscribe to notifications")
- [Use a project badge](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/managing-your-projects/managing-your-project-as-developer/ "Use a project badge")
